quoteLast: . Whatever I write comes to me perfectly easily and naturally, and I rarely ever make any change in my first copy. My mother used to say that I wrote just as other people hemmed handkerchiefs. Writing has never meant any struggle whatever to me.
title: The Women Who Make Our Novels
author: Overton, Grant M. (Grant Martin), 1887-1930
